rulings.Case Title: IN RE: NEMAN BROTHERS AND ASSOCIATES, INC., A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Authorizing The San Francisco County Sheriff To Accept The High Bid Of $425,000.00 Placed At The June 27, 2024 Sheriffs Sale Of 75 Madrone Avenue, San Francisco, Cal. (Ccp 704.800(B)(1))
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 14, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER NEMAN BROTHERS AND ASSOCIATES, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Motion For Order Authorizing The San Francisco County Sheriff To Accept The High Bid Of $425,000.00 Placed At The June 27, 2024 Sheriffs Sale Of 75 Madrone Avenue, San Francisco, Cal. (Ccp 704.800(B)(1)) is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JASON SELLO VS. COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Liza Milanes
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 14, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION DBA COMPASS REAL ESTATE, MONICA PAULI, MARILYN HAYES Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Liza Milanes is GRANTED. Moving party carried their burden under Tech-Built, therefore the cause of action for Implied and/or Equitable Indemnity in the operative cross-complaint is dismissed. All other causes of action in the cross-complaint remain as based, at least in part, on direct liability independent from "liability for contribution or equitable indemnity to any other joint tortfeasor or co-obligor." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH QUINN ET AL VS. MARY R. COULTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant In Support Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs' Third Cause Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 14, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT MARY COULTON Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant In Support Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs' Third Cause Of Action is DENIED. The title of the cause of action does not control and the cause of action stands if allegations therein state any cause of action. It is undisputed that "Each coterminous owner is entitled to the lateral and subjacent support which his land receives from the adjoining land." "All that [section 832] does is to permit a land owner to excavate, freed from the absolute common law right of lateral support in his neighbor, provided certain conditions in the code section are complied with, and always provided that negligence of the excavator is not the proximate cause of damage to the property of the adjoining land owner." Marin Municipal Water Dist. v. Northwestern Pac. R. Co. (1967) 253 Cal.App.2d 83. Excavation is not an issue in this case, so the exceptions provided for in CC 832 are not applicable. At a minimum Plaintiff stated a cause of action for negligence and the title of the third cause of action may be disregarded. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MORPHOS GALLERY, INC., DBA CATHARINE CLARK ET AL VS. DAVID A. KLEIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 14, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT BRIAN GROSS , AN INDIVIDUAL, BRIAN GROSS FINE ART, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to each cause of action. Plaintiff must allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of each cause of action alleged against the moving defendants. All amendments must be in bold font. The Court notes that Plaintiff's opposition is practically devoid of any citations to authorities. The entire opposition references 3 cases: one to discuss general demurer law and two addressing the second cause of action. No authorities supporting Plaintiff's position and no discussion of authorities provided by the moving party are present for the rest of the opposition. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
